questions.q: BrahMos is a missile jointly developed by India and which other country?
questions.explanation: BrahMos is a joint venture between India's DRDO and Russia's NPO Mashinostroyeniya.

questions.q: What type of missile is BrahMos?
questions.explanation: BrahMos is a supersonic cruise missile, among the fastest of its kind in service.

questions.q: The name 'BrahMos' is a combination of the names of which two rivers?
questions.explanation: The name BrahMos is formed from the Brahmaputra River of India and the Moskva River of Russia.

questions.q: Which Indian organisation partners with Russia's NPO Mashinostroyeniya to make BrahMos?
questions.explanation: India's Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) is the Indian partner in the BrahMos joint venture.

questions.q: Roughly what top speed does the BrahMos missile reach?
questions.explanation: BrahMos cruises at roughly Mach 2.8 to 3, making it one of the fastest cruise missiles in service.

questions.q: BrahMos is designed to be launched from which of the following platforms?
questions.explanation: BrahMos is a versatile missile capable of being launched from land, sea, air, and submarine platforms.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of the BrahMos missile?
options: Precision strike against surface and naval targets
questions.explanation: BrahMos is a precision-strike cruise missile used against land and ship targets.

questions.q: The company that produces the missile is named what?
questions.explanation: BrahMos Aerospace is the India-Russia joint venture company that develops and produces the missile.

questions.q: Being 'supersonic' means BrahMos travels faster than what?
questions.explanation: Supersonic means a speed greater than the speed of sound (Mach 1).

questions.q: Which branch of the Indian armed forces was the first to induct BrahMos?
questions.explanation: The Indian Navy was the first service to induct BrahMos, deploying it on warships from 2005.

questions.q: BrahMos is largely derived from which Russian cruise missile?
questions.explanation: BrahMos is based on the Russian P-800 Oniks (Yakhont) anti-ship cruise missile.

questions.q: In which year was the BrahMos Aerospace joint venture formally established?
questions.explanation: BrahMos Aerospace was established in 1998 through an inter-governmental agreement between India and Russia.

questions.q: BrahMos uses a two-stage propulsion system. What kind of engine powers its cruise (sustainer) phase?
questions.explanation: A solid-propellant booster gets it airborne, after which a liquid-fuelled ramjet sustains supersonic cruise.

questions.q: The original land and naval BrahMos had an operational range of approximately how far?
questions.explanation: The original BrahMos had a range of about 290 km, kept under MTCR limits before India joined the regime.

questions.q: After India joined the Missile Technology Control Regime (MTCR) in 2016, the range of BrahMos could be extended toward roughly what figure?
questions.explanation: India's 2016 MTCR membership allowed the range to be extended toward about 450-500 km and beyond in later variants.

questions.q: What flight profile capability lets BrahMos evade detection late in its approach to a target?
questions.explanation: BrahMos can descend to a sea-skimming altitude of just a few metres in its terminal phase to evade radar.

questions.q: Which aircraft was integrated to carry the air-launched BrahMos-A variant?
questions.explanation: The air-launched BrahMos-A was integrated onto the Indian Air Force's Su-30MKI fighter.

questions.q: BrahMos typically carries which kind of warhead?
questions.explanation: BrahMos carries a conventional high-explosive warhead and is a conventional, not nuclear, weapon.

questions.q: Which guidance principle does BrahMos use in its terminal phase to home onto targets?
questions.explanation: BrahMos uses inertial navigation mid-course and an active radar seeker for terminal homing.

questions.q: Why does BrahMos deliver greater kinetic impact than a typical subsonic cruise missile?
options: Its high supersonic speed gives much greater impact energy
It relies only on its small size
questions.explanation: Its high supersonic speed gives BrahMos far greater kinetic impact energy than slow subsonic cruise missiles.

questions.q: Which country became the first foreign customer to sign a major deal to buy the BrahMos missile?
questions.explanation: The Philippines, in 2022, became the first export customer for BrahMos, ordering it for coastal defence.

questions.q: What does the 'cruise' in 'cruise missile' refer to?
options: It flies a sustained, self-propelled, mostly level powered flight to its target
It is carried by a ship only
questions.explanation: A cruise missile flies a sustained, self-propelled, aerodynamically supported flight to its target, unlike a ballistic missile.

questions.q: The first successful test launch of the BrahMos missile took place in which year?
questions.explanation: BrahMos made its first successful test flight in 2001 from a test range on India's coast.

questions.q: What does the suffix in the designation 'P-800 Oniks', the missile BrahMos is based on, broadly indicate about its role?
questions.explanation: The P-800 Oniks is a Russian anti-ship cruise missile, the design lineage from which BrahMos was developed.

questions.q: The MTCR threshold that originally capped the exportable BrahMos range was tied to which payload/range combination?
questions.explanation: MTCR Category I restricts systems able to deliver a 500 kg payload to at least 300 km, which shaped BrahMos's original 290 km range.

questions.q: What is the name of the lighter, smaller next-generation variant of BrahMos being developed for wider platform integration?
questions.explanation: BrahMos-NG (Next Generation) is a smaller, lighter variant intended for a broader range of launch platforms.

questions.q: A hypersonic successor under study, intended to fly much faster than the current missile, is generally referred to as what?
questions.explanation: BrahMos-II is a proposed hypersonic version aimed at speeds well above the current Mach-3 missile.

questions.q: The air-launched BrahMos-A first achieved a successful firing from a Su-30MKI in which year?
questions.explanation: The first successful air-launch of BrahMos from a Su-30MKI took place in November 2017.

questions.q: Compared with the standard ground variant, the air-launched BrahMos-A was modified primarily in which way?
options: It was made heavier with a larger warhead
Its launch booster was reduced/altered and it was lightened for air carriage
It was given a nuclear warhead
It was converted to subsonic flight
questions.explanation: The air-launched variant was lightened and had its booster modified since the aircraft provides initial speed and altitude.

questions.q: Which feature most distinguishes a cruise missile like BrahMos from a ballistic missile such as Agni?
options: It flies a powered, aerodynamic, low-altitude path rather than a high ballistic arc
It can only be launched from space
questions.explanation: Unlike a ballistic missile's high arcing trajectory, BrahMos flies a continuously powered, aerodynamic, mostly low-altitude path.
